zh.lifeSummary: 當Philip ap Jevan Gwilim於1525年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根格萊斯時，他的父親Jevan William25歲，母親Rhys Fitzurynna Thomas16歲。他於大約1550年與Gwenllian  Wanllian verch Gwilym在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根布利特賀迪爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1575年在英國格盧斯特斯爾享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Philip ap Jevan Gwilim于1525年出生在威尔士格拉莫爾根格萊斯时，他的父亲Jevan William25岁，母亲Rhys Fitzurynna Thomas16岁。他于大约1550年与Gwenllian  Wanllian verch Gwilym在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根布利特賀迪爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1575年在英国格盧斯特斯爾享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Jevan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, originally a nickname from Anglo-Norman French jovene ‘young’ (Latin iuvenis). Use as a given name has probably been influenced by its similarity in sound to Evan .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Philip ap Jevan Gwilim was born in 1525, in Glais, Glamorgan, Wales, his father, Jevan William, was 25 and his mother, Rhys Fitzurynna Thomas, was 16. He married Gwenllian  Wanllian verch Gwilym about 1550, in Brithdir, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died in 1575, in Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 50.

Il a épousé Gwenllian  Wanllian verch Gwilym environ 1550, à Brithdir, Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1575, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 50 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Philip ap Jevan Gwilimが1525年にウェールズグラモーガングレイスで生まれた時，父Jevan Williamは25歳，母Rhys Fitzurynna Thomasは16歳であった。約1550年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンブリスディルでGwenllian  Wanllian verch Gwilymと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1575年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Philip ap Jevan Gwilim(이)가 1525년 웨일스 글램르건 글레이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jevan William(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Rhys Fitzurynna Thomas(은)는 16세였다. 그는 대략 1550년 영국 웨일스 글램르건 브리스디르에서 Gwenllian  Wanllian verch Gwilym(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1575년 영국 그루스스터실르에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Philip ap Jevan Gwilim родился в 1525 г. в местности глеи, глэморган, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Jevan William было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Rhys Fitzurynna Thomas, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Gwenllian  Wanllian verch Gwilym около 1550 г. в местности бритдир, глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер в 1575 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 50 лет.
